data.title: REG-Kvika banki hf: Kvika’s Board responds to merger proposals
data.description:
Reference is made to announcements from Kvika banki hf. (“Kvika” or “the Bank”) dated 27 and 28 May, stating that Arion banki hf. and Íslandsbanki hf. had each expressed interest in initiating merger discussions with Kvika.

Following due consideration, the Board of Kvika has concluded that the proposals received from Arion banki hf. and Íslandsbanki hf. on 27 and 28 May do not reflect the intrinsic value of Kvika. The Board therefore does not believe that entering into merger discussion based on these proposals is in the best interest of the bank.

Nonetheless, the Board of Kvika is of the opinion that significant opportunities and value could be realised through a potential combination of businesses, to the benefit of both shareholders and customers. Should the interested parties be willing to improve their proposals, the Board is open to revaluate its decision. Arion banki hf. and Íslandsbanki hf. have been notified accordingly.

Please note that this notice is a disclosure of inside information per article 7 of regulation (EU) No 596/2014 on market abuse (“MAR”), which is implemented into Icelandic law with the act on measures against market abuse No 60/2021.

data.title: Treasury Yields Lifted by Inflation Worries, Consumer Sentiment Data — WSJ
data.description:
By Sam Goldfarb

Treasury yields are higher Friday, with investors calculating that the surge in oil prices could push up overall inflation and make it harder for the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates.

While geopolitical instability can sometimes boost Treasurys as investors look to buy safer assets, bonds are not serving as that haven now partly due to those inflation concerns, according to analysts.

Bonds, though, have also weakened due to more welcome news, with yields extending their morning gains after the University of Michigan's consumer sentiment index rose much more than analysts had expected.

A rebound in consumer sentiment could point to a brighter economic outlook, which also would reduce pressure on the Fed to cut interest rates.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Update: Dundee Precious Metals Gains Near 2% as Agrees to Acquire Adriatic Metals in US$1.3 Billion Deal
data.description:
(Updates shares.)

Dundee Precious Metals on Friday said it agreed to acquire U.K.-based Adriatic Metals in a US$1.3 billion deal.

Dundee will acquire 100% of the Vares operation in Bosnia and Herzegovina, a producing silver-lead-zinc-gold underground mine.

The deal is expected to raise Dundee's production to up to 425,000 gold equivalent ounces in 2027 through the ramp-up of Vares. The mine has an initial 15-year operating life with potential to extend further from a prospective 4,400-hectare land package.

"Vares is a logical fit with our portfolio, as it significantly increases DPM's mine life while adding near-term production growth, a highly prospective land package, and cash flow diversification," said chief executive David Rae.

Under the deal, Adriatic shareholders will receive 0.159 of a Dundee share and 93 pence in cash for each ordinary share of Adriatic. The transaction will be implemented by means of a court-sanctioned scheme of arrangement.

Following deal completion, Dundee's current shareholders will own 75% of the company with the former Adriatic shareholders owning the rest.

Dundee shares were last seen up $0.435 to $20.995 on the Toronto Stock Exchange.

data.title: UK Stocks Retreat on Escalating Middle East Tensions
data.description:
Britain's FTSE 100 index slumped 0.46% at the end of the trading week, joining a global retreat caused by the escalation of the conflict in the Middle East.

Israel on Friday launched large-scale strikes against Iran, targeting the latter's nuclear program. UK officials said the country has no involvement but is continuing to monitor the situation.

"The reports of these strikes are concerning and we urge all parties to step back and reduce tensions urgently," UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer said. "Now is the time for restraint, calm and a return to diplomacy."

Global oil prices climbed amid the Middle East conflict, with British energy majors BP (BP.L) and Shell jumping 1.56% and 0.77%, respectively, on Friday's close.

In corporate news, Oxford Instruments reported a lower attributable profit but higher revenue in the year ended March 31, sending its shares down 2.99%. The technology products and services company also plans to launch a buyback program of up to 50 million pounds sterling.

"Looking ahead, whilst acknowledging the level of macro uncertainty, we have a strong and more focused business; there is a lot we can control, and we are well placed to mitigate any direct impact from tariffs," Chief Executive Officer Richard Tyson said. "We are confident that our differentiated higher margin business will continue to deliver attractive profitable growth."

data.title: Week Ahead for FX, Bonds: Focus on Fed Decision, Middle East Tensions
data.description:
By Dow Jones Newswires Staff

Below are the most important global events likely to affect FX and bond markets in the week starting June 16.

An announcement by the U.S. Federal Reserve will be the focus of the week, where interest rates are expected to be left on hold but investors will watch for any signals on the timing of future rate cuts.

Markets will keep a close eye on developments in the Middle East after Israel's strikes on Iranian nuclear facilities caused sharp falls in equities and a jump in oil prices. Further oil-price rises might prevent many central banks from reducing interest rates.

A number of other central bank decisions are scheduled during the week, including in Japan, China the U.K. and Switzerland.

U.S.

The Federal Reserve is expected to keep interest rates on hold at their current 4.25%-4.50% range in a decision on Wednesday.

Focus will center on any signals on when the central bank could cut interest rates. Recent weaker-then-expected consumer and producer price inflation prompted many in the market to bring forward their expectations for the next rate cut. Money markets fully price in a rate cut in October, with a high chance of this being as early as September, LSEG data show. Previously, markets hadn't fully priced in a rate reduction until December.

Analysts at Citi said markets are underpricing the risk of interest-rate cuts. They pointed to slowing core inflation as well as rising continuing jobless claims which suggest that the labor market is loosening.

However, investors are mindful that U.S. tariffs will likely add to inflation as well as weighing on growth. This could delay rate cuts, especially if tensions between Israel and Iran escalate, causing oil prices to rise further.

"The Fed is unlikely to ease monetary policy during the peak of inflation," said analysts at Allianz Research.

Economic data in the coming week include U.S. retail sales and industrial production for May on Tuesday, followed by May housing starts and weekly jobless claims on Wednesday.

Demand at U.S. Treasury auctions will remain a focus for bond investors.

The Treasury will auction $13 billion in 20-year bonds on Monday and $23 billion in five-year Treasury Inflation-Protected Securities on Tuesday.

Latin America

Brazil's central bank announces a decision on Wednesday. It is expected to leave the Selic rate at 14.75%, although analysts see a chance that it could opt for another 25 basis-point hike to 15.00%.

Eurozone

The upcoming eurozone data calendar is light, leaving the focus on the Eurogroup meeting of eurozone finance ministers on Thursday and Friday.

Italy's second-estimate inflation data for May are scheduled for Monday, followed by Germany's ZEW economic sentiment index for June on Tuesday. Eurozone second-estimate harmonized inflation data for May are scheduled for Wednesday.

German producer price data for May are due on Friday.

Germany will conduct an auction for 2029- and 2033-dated green bonds on Tuesday and for 2046- and 2054-dated Bunds on Wednesday. Other issuers include Slovakia on Monday and Spain and France on Thursday.

U.K.

Focus will center on a Bank of England decision on Thursday. The BOE is widely expected to leave its key interest rate on hold at 4.25%.

Investors will scrutinize the vote breakdown and any commentary on when rates might fall again. The BOE has pointed to a gradual, quarterly pace of reductions recently due to elevated levels of inflation. Most analysts expect it to stick to that guidance for now.

However, recent data have pointed to a weak economy, with data showing that the economy contracted by 0.3% in April while unemployment picked up. This could worry BOE policymakers and investors have increased rate-cut expectations accordingly. Money markets fully price in another rate reduction in September, with a sizeable risk of an earlier cut in August, followed by one more rate cut by the end of the year and another in early 2026.

The uncertain outlook means particular attention will be paid to U.K. inflation data for May due on Wednesday, ahead of the BOE's decision.

Annual inflation rose to 3.5% in April and is expected to remain high for now. Investec expects the rate to drop to 3.3%, particularly after the Office for National Statistics admitted that it overestimated the impact from a rise in vehicle excise duty. If Investec's estimate is correct, this could ease the BOE's concerns about inflation over the summer and pave the way for an interest-rate cut in August, economist Philip Shaw said in a note.

Other data include the GfK's consumer confidence index for June plus retail sales and public finances data on Friday.

The U.K.'s Debt Management Office is due to sell gilts maturing in 2030 on Tuesday.

Scandinavia

Interest-rate decisions are due from Sweden's Riksbank on Wednesday and from Norway's Norges Bank on Thursday.

Analysts are divided as to whether the Riksbank will leave interest rates on hold at 2.25% or whether it will opt for a 25 basis-point reduction to 2.0% in an attempt to shield Sweden's economy from the impact of U.S. tariffs. Money markets price in around a 60% chance of a rate cut, LSEG data show.

Analysts at Nordea have recently changed their view and now forecast a rate cut from the Riksbank, having previously expected unchanged rates. Recent economic growth and inflation data have been lower than it expected, Nordea said in a note.

Norges Bank is expected to leave its policy rate unchanged at 4.5%, with rate cuts not expected until later in the year.

With the economy growing at a decent pace and the labor market still tight, the central bank will probably cut rates quite gradually in the second half of the year, said Jack Allen-Reynolds, deputy chief eurozone economist at Capital Economics, in a note.

Swedish May unemployment data are due Tuesday.

Denmark will hold a bond auction on Wednesday.

Switzerland

The Swiss National Bank is expected to cut interest rates in a decision on Thursday, with the potential that it could opt to take rates back into negative territory due to recent weak inflation data and a strong Swiss franc.

Most expect the SNB to reduce rates by 25 basis points to zero percent. However, there is a risk of it opting for a bigger half-point reduction, taking rates to -0.25%. Money markets show around a 25% chance of this outcome, LSEG data show.

"While the SNB would prefer to avoid deeper cuts, a 50 basis-point reduction in June cannot be ruled out," ING analyst Charlotte de Montpellier said in a note.

Turkey

Turkey's central bank is due to announce a rate decision on Thursday.

Japan

The Bank of Japan is widely expected to keep its policy rate steady at 0.5% at its two-day meeting ending Tuesday. Markets will watch for any changes to the pace of its Japanese government bond purchases reductions.

Under its current plan, the BOJ is cutting its JGB purchases by 400 billion yen per quarter through March 2026. Governor Kazuo Ueda has said yields should be set by markets, following the end of yield curve control.

JPMorgan expects the BOJ to maintain this pace until March 2026, then slow reductions to 200 billion yen per quarter, trimming monthly purchases to about 2.1 trillion yen by March 2027 before halting further cuts.

Inflation data due Friday is likely to show a pick-up, driven by the end of energy subsidies and rising food prices. Core CPI excluding fresh food is expected to rise 3.6% in May from a year earlier, up from 3.5% in April, according to a Quick poll.

Trade data for May and April machinery orders will be released Wednesday. Exports are expected to weaken amid higher U.S. tariffs.

The BOJ is set to buy JGBs on Friday with maturities of 1 to 3, 5 to 10, and 10 to 25 years. The planned purchases are likely to support the domestic bond market. On Thursday, the finance ministry will auction about 2.4 trillion yen in five-year sovereign notes, with investors watching for signs of demand.

China

A data-heavy Monday lies ahead, with May house prices and activity indicators offering a mid-quarter health check on China's economy.

Economists polled The Wall Street Journal expect growth to have softened in May as U.S. trade tensions persisted. Industrial output growth likely slowed slightly to 5.9% from April's 6.1%, while retail sales--a gauge of consumption--likely dipped to 4.9% from 5.1%. Fixed-asset investment is expected to have grown 4.0% over January-May, matching the pace seen in the first four months of the year.

Goldman Sachs economists say May data so far has been mixed, citing weak inflation and underwhelming exports. They expect below-consensus industrial output but stronger-than-expected retail sales.

ING sees indicators holding broadly steady, though with a tilt toward moderation as tariff impacts deepen.

After April's modest housing price rebound on the back of policy support, markets will watch May data for further signs of stabilization in the troubled sector.

On Friday, attention will turn to the announcement of a key lending rate. Following a cut in May, the People's Bank of China is widely expected to announce that the loan prime rate - the benchmark for many household, corporate and property loans - remains steady.

Australia/New Zealand

New Zealand's first-quarter GDP data, due Thursday, will be closely watched for signs of a sustained recovery.

The economy returned to growth in the prior quarter, exiting a period of recession, helped by aggressive interest-rate cuts, but the outlook remains uncertain.

Capital Economics said partial activity indicators suggest a solid first-quarter growth, but warns it is too early to claim that it is out of the woods. Private consumption seems likely to fall sharply this quarter, and migration is unlikely to lift domestic demand.

"All told, we believe the case for the RBNZ to inject further policy stimulus remains intact," CE said.

Philippines

data.title: Lost Money on Compass Group Diversified Holdings, LLC (CODI)? Contact Levi & Korsinsky to Join Class Action Before July 8, 2025
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / June 13, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Compass Group Diversified Holdings, LLC investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/compass-diversified-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=153019&wire=1&utm_campaign=20

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Compass Group Diversified Holdings, LLC that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between May 1, 2024 and May 7, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: According to the complaint, throughout the class period, defendants failed to disclose to investors that Compass lacked effective internal controls over its financial reporting; that Compass failed to disclose critical information regarding Lugano Holding, Inc. ("Lugano") which kept undisclosed financing arrangements and exhibited irregularities in its sales, cost of sales, inventory and accounts receivable; and that, as a result of the foregoing, defendants' positive statements about the Company's financial reporting were materially misleading.

The truth emerged on May 7, 2025, after the market closed, the Company announced that its financial statements for fiscal 2024 could no longer be relied upon due to an ongoing internal investigation into its subsidiary, Lugano. Specifically, Compass reported that its Audit Committee launched an investigation over "concerns about how Lugano was potentially financing inventory." The Company also announced that it intends to delay the filing of its first quarter 2025 Form 10-Q. Further, effective May 7, 2025, Lugano's founder and CEO, Moti Ferder, resigned from Lugano and will not receive any severance compensation.

Following this news, the price of Compass' common stock declined dramatically. From a closing market price of $17.25 per share on May 7, 2025 to $6.55 per share on May 8, 2025.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Compass Diversified stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/compass-diversified-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=153019&wire=1&utm_campaign=20 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

data.title: Oil Advances After Israel Attacks Iran; ING Sees Crude Approaching $80 if Conflict Escalates
data.description:
Oil prices jumped Friday after Israel targeted Iran's nuclear facilities in a series of airstrikes, with ING Bank saying that an escalation could push prices above $80 a barrel.

Israel attacked Iran in the early hours of Friday local time, targeting military infrastructure and nuclear facilities, according to several media publications. The strikes killed Mohammad Hossein Bagheri, chief of the Iranian Armed Forces, and Hossein Salami, the commander-in-chief of Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.

Iran sent about 100 drones into Israel in retaliation, CNBC reported, citing an Israeli military spokesperson.

US President Donald Trump urged Iran to reach a nuclear deal with Washington, according to his Friday posts on the Truth Social platform. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the US was not involved in the operation.

West Texas Intermediate crude rose 6.1% to $72.27 a barrel in Friday trade, while Brent climbed 6% to $73.52.

ING estimates that Iran produces about 3.3 million barrels of crude oil per day and exports 1.7 million barrels in the region. An escalation in the Israel-Iran conflict that leads to a loss of Iranian oil flows could drive prices toward $80 a barrel, potentially reaching $120 if shipping through the Strait of Hormuz is also impacted, ING said in a report published Friday.

Absent the more severe scenario, crude prices could settle in a range of $75 to $80, ING said. The Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries, or OPEC, could release additional supply sooner than expected, if needed.

Higher oil prices are likely to hinder the progress on inflation in the US, ING said.

Earlier in the week, government data showed that consumer inflation in the world's largest economy unexpectedly decelerated in May, weighed down by lower energy prices.

"So far, goods inflation has stayed remarkably calm, while price pressures within services, which represent three-quarters of the core CPI basket, have begun to ease," according to ING. "We don't think that will last. Inventory buffers may have allowed firms to put off decisions about raising prices, but that won't be the case for much longer."

President Donald Trump said Wednesday that the inflation report showed "great numbers" and that the Federal Reserve should lower its policy rate by a full percentage point.

Higher oil prices will keep the central bank from lowering rates in the third quarter, according to ING, which projects the Fed to make its first cut of the year in December. The impact of Trump's tariffs on inflation are expected to begin easing as the second half of the year progresses, while the economic hit will likely become "more apparent in areas like unemployment," ING wrote.

Markets are widely anticipating the Fed to leave its benchmark lending rate unchanged next week, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

data.title: Augusta Precious Metals Reviews: Best Gold IRA Company of 2025? (New Report Released)
data.description:

NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / June 13, 2025 / Searching for reliable Augusta Precious Metals reviews? Of course, we get it. Augusta Precious Metals ranks as America's #1 Precious Metals company with more than 1,000 five-star ratings on consumer platforms of all sizes. Money magazine named them the "Best Overall Gold IRA Company" from 2022 to 2025.

Their spotless record since 2012 stands out in Augusta Precious Metals review content. This gold IRA provider has managed to keep zero complaints with the Better Business Bureau (BBB) and Business Consumer Alliance (BCA). Their A+ and AAA ratings reflect this excellence. Customer reviews consistently praise the company's transparency and dedication to client satisfaction.

Get a One-on-One Web Conference with Augusta Precious Metals

Alternatively, read the full Augusta Precious Metals review here.

Augusta Precious Metals IRA services shine through their educational focus. Clients benefit from a free gold IRA guide and weekly market updates. They also get access to private web conferences with a Harvard-educated economist. Each customer receives lifetime account support from a dedicated customer success agent throughout their investment trip.

In this piece, we'll get into the pros and cons of partnering with this revolutionary force. You'll learn about their gold and silver products and discover why customers seeking precious metals expertise hold them in such high regard.

What Is Augusta Precious Metals and Why It Stands Out

Augusta Precious Metals has become a specialized investment firm with a unique position in the precious metals industry. While competitors try to broaden into various investment options, Augusta manages to keep its expertise focused on gold and silver investments. This specialized approach gives their clients deeper knowledge and better service.

Founded in 2012: A Focus on Gold and Silver

Isaac Nuriani started Augusta Precious Metals in 2012 with a clear vision to enable people to broaden their wealth through precious metals. This Beverly Hills, California-based family business shows a steadfast dedication to gold and silver investments that spans over a decade. Other companies expanded into cryptocurrency or traditional IRAs, but Augusta chose to specialize in precious metals.

This single focus lets Augusta deliver deeper expertise, stronger security, and better client service. They aim to educate and help Americans balance their retirement savings through physical precious metals. Augusta's deep knowledge of gold and silver helps retirement savers reduce market volatility and economic uncertainty risks.

Trusted by Thousands: BBB, BCA, and Consumer Ratings

Trust matters most in the precious metals industry, and Augusta's record speaks for itself. They have zero complaints with both the Better Business Bureau and Business Consumer Alliance since 2012. This clean record earned them an A+ rating from the BBB and a prestigious AAA rating from the BCA.

Augusta's customer satisfaction scores shine across multiple platforms:

  • TrustLink: Perfect 5.0 rating based on 288 reviews

  • Google My Business: 4.9 rating from 493 reviews

  • Consumer Affairs: 5.0 rating based on 141 reviews

  • Facebook: 96% rating from 88 reviews

Augusta Precious Metals also won Stevie Awards for Customer Service Success and Sales Excellence. Money magazine named them "Best Overall Gold IRA Company" from 2022 to 2025.

Devlyn Steele, Augusta's education director and Harvard-trained economic analyst, leads their educational efforts. His expertise helps customers understand economic trends and make smart precious metals investment decisions. This focus on education, combined with their strong track record, explains why Augusta Precious Metals reviews rank them among America's most trusted gold IRA providers.

Learn More About Augusta Gold IRA Here.

Augusta Gold IRA: How the Process Works

Setting up a Gold IRA with Augusta Precious Metals takes just four simple steps. The company makes it easy to secure your retirement assets. My research of Augusta Precious Metals reviews shows they put their clients first. Their representatives stay with you through each step of the setup process.

Step 1: Open a Self-Directed IRA

Let's start by setting up a self-directed IRA for your physical precious metals. Augusta gives you a personal customer success agent who becomes your main contact. This person helps you fill out the forms needed to open your account with Augusta's preferred custodian. Augusta gold ira reviews show their team handles about 95% of the paperwork, which makes things much easier for you.

Augusta has carefully chosen their custodians. They work mostly with Equity Trust, the leading self-directed IRA provider in the nation. Equity Trust brings over 30 years of experience and manages more than $25 billion in assets.

Step 2: Transfer or Rollover Retirement Funds

Your next step is to fund your new self-directed IRA. Most people move their money from existing retirement accounts like traditional IRAs, Roth IRAs, 401(k)s, or 403(b)s. This method works well because it keeps your tax advantages intact.

Augusta's team makes this crucial phase worry-free. Their IRA processing team works directly with your current retirement account custodian. They handle all the communication and paperwork. Remember that IRS rules give you 60 days to complete rollovers to avoid tax issues.

Step 3: Choose Your Gold or Silver Products

After funding your account, Augusta's order desk helps you pick IRA-eligible precious metals. Their product specialists show you all your options and help you build a diverse portfolio. Augusta precious metals ira accounts let you invest in several IRS-approved products:

  • American Gold Eagles (1 oz, 1/2 oz, 1/4 oz, and 1/10 oz denominations)

  • American Silver Eagles

  • Other qualifying gold and silver coins and bars

Augusta precious metals pros and cons become clear during selection. They teach rather than sell, and let you make your own choices without pressure.

Step 4: Secure Storage or Home Delivery Options

The last step stores your investment in an IRS-approved depository. Augusta partners with the Delaware Depository, which offers:

  • $1 billion all-risk insurance through London underwriters

  • Top-security facilities approved by exchanges and IRS-compliant

  • Locations in 11 U.S. cities including Los Angeles, Salt Lake City, Las Vegas, and New York

IRA investments must stay in approved storage to keep their tax benefits. For non-IRA precious metals, Augusta can ship them securely to your home. You can also work with your custodian to pick up your IRA metals from storage as part of a distribution.

Augusta precious metals teaches you every step of the way. They help you understand both how gold IRAs work and why precious metals might strengthen your retirement portfolio.

Educational Support That Builds customer Confidence

Education is the life-blood of Augusta Precious Metals' approach to customer relations. My analysis of Augusta Precious Metals reviews shows that customers praise their dedication to building informed customers above all other service aspects.

Free Gold IRA Guide and Video Library

Augusta's detailed gold IRA information kit helps many customers start their precious metals experience. This resource package shows how physical gold and silver can protect portfolios during economic uncertainty. Customers love the kit's simple explanations of IRA regulations that avoid overwhelming technical jargon.

The augusta precious metals video library stands out with its professional production quality and informative content. These resources cover everything from simple precious metals concepts to advanced market analysis. A satisfied customer pointed out that "Educational videos, professionally done" was one of their main reasons for satisfaction. These materials show Augusta's belief that informed clients make better long-term investment decisions.

Get Their Free Gold IRA Guide Here

One-on-One Web Conferences with Experts

Augusta gold ira sets itself apart with its personalized web conference experience. These one-on-one sessions target serious customers with at least $100,000 in savings and have drawn thousands of Americans who want to learn about portfolio diversification strategies.

These interactive sessions teach participants about:

  • How gold and silver function as inflation hedges

  • Federal Reserve policies and their effects on retirement savings

  • Potential risks regarding the U.S. dollar's future

  • Ways to spot and avoid high-pressure sales tactics

Many customers call these conferences crucial to their decision-making process. One Augusta Precious Metals review emphasized, "Every step of the process was explained fully and in a way to make sure I could understand".

Get a One-on-One Web Conference with Augusta Precious Metals

Harvard-Trained Economist Devlyn Steele's Insights

Devlyn Steele leads Augusta's educational approach as their director of education with over 30 years of financial services experience. He has processed financial assets worth more than $2 billion throughout his career. His Harvard Business School analytics program membership adds credibility to Augusta's economic insights.

Steele's expertise has caught notable attention-including hall of fame quarterback Joe Montana, who became Augusta's paid ambassador after attending one of these educational sessions. Steele focuses on Federal Reserve policies that might affect the dollar, gold, and silver.

Weekly Market Updates and Scam Awareness Resources

Augusta builds customer confidence through regular market updates that help clients understand precious metals price influences. This ongoing education continues well after the original investments, so clients can understand market movements without doing extensive research themselves.

Augusta stands out by maintaining a dedicated section about spotting and avoiding investment scams-a feature customers find valuable. Their openness about industry pitfalls shows their steadfast dedication to client education, including potential risks. Reports like "10 Gold Dealer Lies" and "15 Bad Reasons to Buy Gold" help customers spot misleading tactics that sometimes appear in the industry.

My research of Augusta Precious Metals pros and cons consistently shows this educational foundation as their most distinctive strength. They create informed customers who make confident decisions based on knowledge rather than pressure.

Learn More About Augusta Precious Metals Here.

Customer Experience: Lifetime Support and No Pressure

Augusta Precious Metals built its stellar reputation on a customer service model that values lasting relationships over quick sales. Their reviews consistently show how customers appreciate the genuine support they get with their gold investments.

Dedicated Customer Success Agents

New Augusta clients get their own customer success agent who becomes their main contact. This agent works as your connection to other departments and stays available by phone or email to answer questions throughout your investment experience. You won't need to repeat your story to different representatives because your agent knows your portfolio and priorities.

Support from Setup to Buyback

Augusta sets itself apart by giving clients lifetime account support-a big difference from competitors who often vanish after the original sale. Their ongoing help covers:

  • Paperwork for contributions, distributions, and required minimum distributions

  • Additional precious metals purchases

  • Precious metals buybacks when you want to liquidate

Your customer success agent keeps providing valuable resources well after setting up your account and connects you with specialized departments as needed.

No-Commission Sales Approach

Unlike the high-pressure tactics common in this industry, Augusta uses a refreshing no-commission sales model. Their team first understands your financial situation before making tailored recommendations. This educational style lets you make informed decisions without feeling rushed or pressured into buying.

Real User Testimonials and Satisfaction Stories

Augusta has earned a 4.96/5 star rating on BBB with thousands of verified 5-star reviews that praise their "no-pressure conversations" and "clear guidance". Clients often mention Augusta's knowledgeable staff who "take whatever time is necessary to explain everything". This excellent feedback shows up on many platforms, where customers describe their experience as "thorough and seamless" and value Augusta's focus on education over sales pressure.

Augusta Precious Metals vs Other Gold IRA Companies

Augusta Precious Metals stands out from other gold IRA providers in several key areas. Reviews of Augusta Precious Metals among other industry competitors show clear differences in their business approach, history, and customer safety measures.

Specialization in Precious Metals Only

Augusta excels by focusing only on gold and silver products, while most competitors try to broaden into cryptocurrencies, stocks, or other investments. Their dedicated focus helps them master precious metals markets instead of splitting their expertise between different investment types. Companies that offer various metals or crypto IRAs can't match Augusta's competitive edge and deep market knowledge. This laser focus helped them earn Money Magazine's "Best Overall Gold IRA Company" title for 2022-2024, even with tough competition.

No Complaints Record Since 2012

The most remarkable aspect in Augusta gold IRA reviews is their spotless complaint history. Augusta has managed to keep zero complaints with the Better Business Bureau and Business Consumer Alliance since they started in 2012. No other gold IRA company has matched this achievement. The gold IRA sector often faces criticism for pushy sales tactics, but Augusta's educational approach helps them avoid the customer satisfaction problems that plague their competitors.

Price Match Guarantee and Buyback Program

Augusta can't legally promise to buy back products, but they've never rejected a buyback request. They also offer a special "Highest Buyback Price Guarantee" - customers can cancel their buyback trade if they find a better offer within 24 hours. Market ups and downs aside, reviews of Augusta's pros and cons consistently highlight this customer-friendly policy as a major advantage. Many competitors don't provide such strong protections.

Final Thoughts: Why Augusta Precious Metals Stands Above the Rest

I've really analyzed Augusta Precious Metals from every angle and they've earned their spot as America's top gold IRA provider. Their laser focus on gold and silver investments gives them a clear advantage over competitors who spread themselves thin across many investment types. This specialization helps Augusta maintain deeper market knowledge and offer better guidance to customers.

Their spotless record since 2012 impresses me the most. They haven't received a single complaint with the BBB and BCA in over a decade - that's remarkable. Their educational approach makes them special. Instead of pushing sales, they help customers learn through one-on-one web conferences, weekly updates, and resources created by Harvard-trained economist Devlyn Steele.

Augusta's four-step process makes precious metals investing available to everyone, even first-time gold IRA customers. Their dedicated customer success agents provide personal support throughout your investment trip, not just when you make your first purchase.

Many gold IRA companies make big promises, but Augusta delivers with substance. They've earned prestigious endorsements from Joe Montana, Money magazine's "Best Overall Gold IRA Company" title for four straight years, and thousands of five-star reviews everywhere. Their price match guarantee and buyback program show how confident they are in their value. Augusta Precious Metals gives serious customers everything they need: deep expertise, clear practices, ongoing education, and lifetime support. Looking to vary your retirement portfolio or guard against economic uncertainty? Augusta offers a trusted path to precious metals investment that's nowhere near what other companies provide.

Be sure to get a One-on-One Web Conference with Augusta Precious Metals

FAQs

Q1. What makes Augusta Precious Metals stand out among gold IRA companies? Augusta Precious Metals specializes exclusively in gold and silver investments, offering deeper expertise and personalized service. They have maintained a spotless record with zero complaints since 2012 and provide extensive educational resources, including one-on-one web conferences with experts.

Q2. How does the Augusta Gold IRA process work? The process involves four steps: opening a self-directed IRA, transferring or rolling over retirement funds, choosing gold or silver products, and securing storage. Augusta provides dedicated customer support throughout, handling most of the paperwork and coordinating with custodians to ensure a smooth setup.

Q3. What educational resources does Augusta Precious Metals offer? Augusta offers a free Gold IRA guide, a comprehensive video library, one-on-one web conferences with experts, insights from Harvard-trained economist Devlyn Steele, weekly market updates, and scam awareness resources. These materials help customers make informed decisions about precious metals investments.

Q4. Does Augusta Precious Metals offer ongoing support after the initial investment? Yes, Augusta provides lifetime account support through dedicated customer success agents. They assist with paperwork, additional purchases, and even buybacks when you're ready to liquidate. This ongoing support is a key feature that sets them apart from many competitors.

Q5. How does Augusta Precious Metals protect its customers' investments? Augusta offers a unique "Highest Buyback Price Guarantee" and has never turned down a buyback request. They also use secure, IRS-approved depositories for storage, with facilities insured for up to $1 billion. Additionally, their no-pressure, educational approach helps customers make informed decisions rather than rushed purchases.

About IRAEmpire.com

IRAEmpire.com is a trusted source for retirement-focused financial insights and investment reviews. Founded in 2020, the platform evaluates a wide range of retirement products including Gold IRAs, 401(k) rollovers, crypto retirement accounts, and traditional brokerage options.

The site's research methodology involves hundreds of hours of analysis across multiple categories, emphasizing transparency, service quality, and value. IRAEmpire.com operates independently and is not influenced by paid sponsorships in its editorial rankings.

Beyond rankings, the site features in-depth guides on asset protection, inflation hedging, and long-term retirement planning-making it a go-to resource for serious customers.

data.title: REG-Aramis Group - 2025 half-year financial report release
data.description:
PRESS RELEASE

Arcueil, June 13, 2025

2025 half-year financial report release

Aramis Group today announces that the company has filed its 2025 half-year financial report, ended March 31, 2025, with the French Financial Markets Authority (Autorité des marchés financiers).

Aramis Group 2025 half-year financial report is available on the Company’s website www.aramis.group under “Investors / Regulated information”.

***

About Aramis Group – www.aramis.group

Aramis Group is the European leader for B2C online used car sales and operates in six countries. A fast-growing group, an e-commerce expert and a vehicle refurbishing pioneer, Aramis Group takes action each day for more sustainable mobility with an offering that is part of the circular economy. Founded in 2001, it has been revolutionizing its market for over 20 years, focused on ensuring the satisfaction of its customers and capitalizing on digital technology and employee engagement to create value for all its stakeholders. With annual revenues of more than €2 billion, Aramis Group sells more than 110,000 vehicles B2C and welcomes close to seventy million visitors across all its digital platforms each year. The Group employs more than 2,400 people and has eight industrial-scale refurbishing centres throughout Europe. Aramis Group is listed on Euronext Paris Compartment B (Ticker: ARAMI – ISIN: FR0014003U94).

Investors contact                                                          Press contacts

investor@aramis.group                                                   Brunswick

Hugues Boëton

Tristan Roquet Montegon

aramisgroup@brunswickgroup.com

+33 (0)6 79 99 27 15

data.description:
Tietoevry

Tietoevry Corporation          STOCK EXCHANGE RELEASE          13 June 2025  6.45 p.m. EET

Tietoevry Corporation shares have been acquired according to the repurchasing programme of the company's own shares in relation to its share-based incentive programmes for key personnel.

Date:                     13 June 2025

Exchange transaction:    Buy

Share class:                         TIETO

Amount, shares:               45 000

Average price/share, EUR             15.9903

Total price, EUR:               719 563.50

The number of shares held by Tietoevry Corporation after the completion of transactions: 113 572 shares.

On behalf of Tietoevry Corporation     

EVLI PLC

Aleksi Jalava

For further information, please contact:

Tommi Järvenpää, Head of Investor Relations, tel. +358 40 576 0288, tommi.jarvenpaa (at) tietoevry.com

TIETOEVRY CORPORATION

DISTRIBUTION

NASDAQ Helsinki

NASDAQ Stockholm

Oslo Børs

Principal Media

Tietoevry is a leading software and digital engineering services company with global market reach and capabilities. We provide customers across different industries with mission-critical solutions through our specialized software businesses* Tietoevry Care, Tietoevry Banking and Tietoevry Industry, as well as our digital engineering business Tietoevry Create. Our 16 000* talented vertical software, design, cloud and AI experts are dedicated to empowering our customers to succeed and innovate with latest technology.

Tietoevry's annual revenue for the continuing businesses* is approximately EUR 2 billion. The company's shares are listed on the NASDAQ exchange in Helsinki and Stockholm, as well as on Oslo Børs. www.tietoevry.com

* Tietoevry Tech Services is excluded due to the divestment signed in March 2025. The transaction is expected to close during Q3 2025.

data.title: REA Holdings Unit Concludes Sale of Cipta Davia Mandiri
data.description:
REA Holdings' (RE.L) PT REA Kaltim Plantations completed the sale of its PT Cipta Davia Mandiri business after all deal conditions were met, according to a Friday release.

The oil palm cultivation company sold the business to PT Teladan Prima Agro Tbk for $23.8 million under a conditional agreement announced April 22.

data.title: BRODSKY & SMITH SHAREHOLDER UPDATE: Notifying Investors of the Following Investigations: CureVac N.V. (Nasdaq - CVAC), Know Labs, Inc. (NYSE American - KNW), Volato Group, Inc. (NYSE American - SOAR), Heliogen, Inc. (OTC - HLGN)
data.description:
BALA CYNWYD, Pa., June 13, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Brodsky & Smith reminds investors of the following investigations. If you own shares and wish to discuss the investigation, contact Jason Brodsky (jbrodsky@brodskysmith.com) or Marc Ackerman (mackerman@brodskysmith.com) at 855-576-4847. There is no cost or financial obligation to you.

CureVac N.V. (Nasdaq - CVAC)

Under the terms of the Merger Agreement, CureVac will be acquired by BioNTech SE (Nasdaq – BNTX). Each CureVac share will be exchanged for approx. $5.46 in BioNTech ADSs, resulting in an implied aggregate equity value for CureVac of approx. $1.25 billion. Upon closing of the transaction, CureVac shareholders are expected to own between 4% and 6% of BioNTech. The investigation concerns whether the CureVac Board breached its fiduciary duties to shareholders by failing to conduct a fair process, including whether the consideration provides fair value to the Company’s shareholders.

Additional information can be found at https://www.brodskysmith.com/cases/curevac-n-v-nasdaq-cvac/.

Know Labs, Inc. (NYSE American - KNW)

Under the terms of the agreement, Know Labs will be acquired by Goldeneye 1995 LLC (“Buyer”), an affiliate of fintech investor, entrepreneur, and former Ripple Chief Risk Officer, Greg Kidd. Buyer will acquire that number of shares of the Company’s common stock obtained by dividing (i) the sum of 1,000 Bitcoin and a cash sum to retire existing debt, redeem outstanding preferred equity and provide additional working capital, by (ii) the per share purchase price of $0.335. The investigation concerns whether the Know Labs Board breached its fiduciary duties to shareholders by failing to conduct a fair process, including whether the deal provides fair value to the Company’s shareholders.

Additional information can be found at https://www.brodskysmith.com/cases/know-labs-inc-nyse-american-knw/.

Volato Group, Inc. (NYSE American - SOAR)

Under the terms of the agreement, Volato Group will merge with M2i Global, Inc. (“M2i Global”) (OTC - MTWO). M2i Global will receive common shares of Volato Group stock such that M2i Global will own approximately 90% of the total shares of common stock of Volato. The investigation concerns whether the Volato Group Board breached its fiduciary duties to shareholders by failing to conduct a fair process, including the dilution to the Company’s shareholders in the combined company.

Additional information can be found at https://www.brodskysmith.com/cases/volato-group-inc-nyse-american-soar/.

Heliogen, Inc. (OTC - HLGN)

Under the terms of the Merger Agreement, Heliogen will be acquired by Zeo Energy Corp. (“Zeo Energy”). Upon the closing of the transaction, Heliogen’s securityholders will receive shares of Zeo’s Class A common stock valued at approximately $10 million in the aggregate, based on a Zeo Class A common stock price of $1.5859 per share, and subject to an adjustment mechanism based on Heliogen’s net cash at the closing. The investigation concerns whether the Heliogen Board breached its fiduciary duties to shareholders by failing to conduct a fair process, including whether the consideration provides fair value to the Company’s shareholders.

Additional information can be found at . https://www.brodskysmith.com/cases/heliogen-inc-otc-hlgn/,

Brodsky & Smith is a litigation law firm with extensive expertise representing shareholders throughout the nation in securities and class action lawsuits. The attorneys at Brodsky & Smith have been appointed by numerous courts throughout the country to serve as lead counsel in class actions and have successfully recovered millions of dollars for our clients and shareholders. Attorney advertising. Prior results do not guarantee a similar outcome.

data.title: ICE Canola Prices Increase on Middle East Tensions, Biofuels
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Canola futures on the Intercontinental Exchange gained strength from surging crude oil prices and reports of new biofuel proposals from the U.S.

Israel launched attacks on Iran on Thursday night, which caused crude oil prices to rise by as much as US$5 per barrel.

Earlier today, there were news reports the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency will submit proposals which include a higher proportion of biofuels than expected. An analyst said these would create greater demand for vegetable oils. Chicago soyoil traded up its daily limit of three U.S. cents per pound.

European rapeseed and Malaysian palm oil also traded higher.

The Canadian dollar was up one-tenth of a U.S. cent compared to Thursday's close.

About 85,900 canola contracts have traded at 10:15 CDT.

 
Prices in Canadian dollars per metric ton: 
 
Canola     Price        Change 
 Jul       732.50       up 8.70 
 Nov       721.70       up 13.50 
 Jan       729.00       up 13.50 
 Mar       732.60       up 11.50

Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com
